segments.s684-g57: همی‌بود is an older epic imperfective/past-continuous form meaning "kept being / remained." تا introduces a temporal subordinate clause "until." خور is the sun; شد (the past of شدن) means "became," and اندر نهفت literally "entered into hiding / was concealed." Together خور شد اندر نهفت = "the sun became hidden" (i.e., set).
segments.s684-g58: بیامد is a narrative past "came" (here implying he went/arrived to the place of rest). پر اندیشه is an adjectival phrase "full of thought" placed before the noun (poetic word order) and modifies دل "heart." بخفت is an older past form of "to sleep" (بـ- prefix + خفت), so دل بخفت literally "the heart slept," idiomatically "he fell asleep" — i.e. he fell asleep with a heart full of thought.
segments.s684-g49: فداکردمش is a past verb phrase: فداکردم “I sacrificed/gave up” plus the enclitic -ش “for him/for it,” giving “I gave my life for him.” جان is the direct noun ‘life’ placed after the verb for emphasis. وبایست کرد is a coordinated verbal phrase: و + بایست (an older/poetic stem meaning ‘stand/hold/stand firm’) with کرد as a light verb, so together ‘and (I) stood/held firm.’
segments.s684-g50: تو‌گر is the conditional particle phrase ‘تو گر’ = ‘if you’. مهتری here names superiority/preeminence (‘greater/one of higher rank’). گرد is a verbal element here (poetic/older usage) functioning like ‘become’/‘turn’ (verb). کژی is a noun meaning ‘crookedness/wrongness,’ and مگرد is a negative/avoidance form meaning ‘do not become / do not turn (into).’ The whole clause is: ‘If you are higher (or claim superiority), do not become crooked/unjust.’
segments.s684-g51: بدو گفت is the sequence ‘to him (بدو) said (گفت)’ — a reporting frame ‘he said to him’. بهرام is the speaker’s name in the report (vocative/subject). من begins the direct speech (‘I …’). زین is an archaic demonstrative/introductory particle ‘from/for/this’ attached to the following noun; here it introduces the noun گناه ‘sin/blame.’
segments.s684-g52: که starts the subordinate clause ‘that/which’. کردی is the simple past ‘you did’ (the action you committed). نخواهمت is future-negation with the enclitic object -ت: نخواهم + ت = ‘I will not (have/choose) you’ — here idiomatically ‘I will not have you made / I will not cause your ruin.’ کردن is the light infinitive/causative ‘to make/do’ attached to تباه ‘ruined/corrupt,’ so together ‘I will not have you made ruined’ = ‘I will not destroy you.’
segments.s684-g53: ولیکن is the adversative ‘but/yet’. تو هم places contrastive emphasis ‘you also/even you’. کشته here is a predicative adjective/participial form ‘slain/killed,’ and بر دست اوی is the agent phrase ‘by his hand’ (بر = by/on, دست = hand/power, اوی = archaic third-person pronoun ‘his’); شوی (in the next segment) completes the verbal idea ‘you will become/be (in that state)’. The sequence therefore means ‘but you too will be slain by his hand.’
segments.s684-g54: شوی is the second-person singular present/future of شدن ‘become’ (‘you will be’). زود means ‘soon/quickly’. و here links the clauses ‘and’. خوانی is a future-form of خواندن in the sense ‘to call/summon’ (i.e. ‘you will be called’); مرا is the object pronoun ‘me’ and را is the definite/direct-object marker attached to it, while راست گوی is a two-word predicate ‘speak straight / tell the truth’ (راست = true/straight, گوی = say/speaking). So: ‘you will soon be slain by his hand; and when you are summoned, tell me the truth.’
segments.s684-g55: نهادند is the past plural verb ‘they placed/they set’ (نهاد being the verbal root). بر پای is the prepositional phrase ‘upon the foot/feet’. بندوی is the captive/prisoner (a bound person, ‘bondman’), and بند is the fetter/bond placed on the feet. The clause means ‘they put a fetter on the prisoner’s feet.’
segments.s684-g56: ببهرام = به + بهرام ‘to/for Bahram’ (they delivered him to Bahram). دادش is داد + -ش ‘they gave him’ (داد used as the verb ‘gave’ here). ز بهر is the idiom ‘for the sake of / for the purpose of’ (ز = from/because of, بهر = for/toward) and گزند means ‘harm/damage.’ Together: ‘and they gave him to Bahram to guard him from harm.’
segments.s684-g25: به + indirect object: به بندوی = 'to the bondman/servant.' گفت is the past verb 'said' governing the quotation that follows. ای introduces a vocative ('O...'), بد is an epithet meaning 'wretched/bad' modifying چاره‌جوی 'seeker of remedy' (a compound noun).
segments.s684-g26: تو is the direct addressee 'you.' این داوریها is the direct object 'these judgments/decisions.' ببهرام is the prepositional phrase 'to/for Bahram' (be- + proper name). گوی here functions as an imperative/command 'say/tell' (poetic/archaic form of گفتن).
segments.s684-g27: فرود آمد is a compound verbal idea meaning 'descended' (فرود 'alighting/fall' + آمد 'came'). از بام بندوی = 'from the bondman's roof' (از + noun + possessor after the noun, typical Persian word order). شیر is the subject 'lion' referenced after the location phrase.
segments.s684-g28: همی‌راند is an imperfective/habitual past form 'was riding/was driving' (poetic form). با here marks accompaniment/agent 'with' (or 'by' in the sense of agency), and نامدار دلیر is a postpositive epithet 'the renowned, brave (one)' that performs the action.
segments.s684-g29: چوبشنید is a fused temporal clause (چو = 'when' + شنید = 'he heard') meaning 'when he heard.' بهرام is the proper name 'Bahram.' کامد is an archaic/poetic past form 'came/arrived.' سپاه is the noun 'army/host' and here indicates Bahram's force coming with him or appearing.
segments.s684-g30: سوی روم شد = 'turned/set (his face) toward Rome' (سوی = 'toward', روم = 'Rome/Byzantium'). شد is the past auxiliary/verb 'became/turned' indicating the change of direction or movement; خسرو is the subject 'Khosrow' placed after the verb for poetic inversion. کینه خواه is a compound: کینه 'hatred/grudge' + خواه 'desirer/wanting', i.e. 'desirous of vengeance.'
segments.s684-g31: زپور سیاوش = 'Zupur (at) Siavash' with بر آشفت سخت expressing sudden/strong emotion: بر + آشفت (آشفت here 'was disturbed/angered') = 'rose up against/was enraged at.' سخت intensifies the verb: 'severely/strongly' (poetic adverb).
segments.s684-g32: بدو = 'to him' (dative). گفت is the past verb 'said' introducing direct speech; here the senses list lacks the verb sense so it is marked accordingly. کای is the vocative 'O!', بدرگ is an epithet 'ill‑colored/pale' addressing appearance or state, and شوربخت is 'ill‑fated/unfortunate' — together a brusque insult: 'O pale, ill‑fated one!'
segments.s684-g33: نه at the start negates the following nominal clause: 'نه کار تو بود' = 'that was not your deed/doing.' کار تو is head noun + possessor 'you.' بود is the past copula ('was'). اینک is an adverb 'now' and فرمودمت is 1st person past ('I told/commanded you'), continuing the speaker's action.
segments.s684-g34: همی is an emphatic/adverbial particle here ('indeed/so'); بی‌هنر is an adjective 'unskilled' modifying the (implied) speaker; خیره is a predicative/adverbial 'astonished, staring'; بستودمت is 1st person past 'I praised you.' The clause links to the previous 'I told you' as another comment by the same speaker.
segments.s684-g35: جهانجوی is a compound adjective/noun 'world-seeking, ambitious' modifying بندوی 'bondman/devotee'; را is the definite object marker attached to that object; پیش functions as a locative/adverbial 'before/in the presence of'; خواند is 3rd-person past 'he summoned/called.'
segments.s684-g36: همی is a narrative/adverbial particle ('then/indeed'); خشم بهرام is a genitive phrase 'Bahram's anger' (anger as subject); با او is a prepositional phrase here ('with/against him' — marking the person affected); براند is 3rd-person present/past-narrative of راندن 'drive away.' Together: 'Bahram's anger (then) drove him away.'
segments.s684-g37: بدو is the prepositional/clitic form 'to him' (he said to him); گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here rendered as the speech-act verb); کای is the vocative particle 'O'; بدتن بدکنش is a two-word vocative 'ill-bodied, ill-doer' addressing the addressee.
segments.s684-g38: فریبنده modifies مرد: 'the deceitful/man who deceives.' از در is a locative phrase 'at/from the gate/door' (here 'at the gate'); سرزنش is a noun/verbal noun 'reproach' used as the predication 'he reproached (him).' Together: 'he reproached the deceitful man at the gate.'
segments.s684-g39: سپاه is the noun 'army/host.' Here سپاه مرا (m + را = مرا split) functions as the object 'my host/army' (possessive/accusative). خیره is an adjectival/adverbial 'astonished/dumbfounded,' and بفریفتی is 2nd-person past 'you deceived/charmed.' The clause addresses the addressee: 'You charmed/bewildered my army.'
segments.s684-g40: زبد is 'the cream/choicest part' placed before the possessed noun as a partitive 'the best of...'; گوهر خوی(ش) = 'the jewel/essence of your nature' (خوی + ش is the genitival 'your'); نشکیفتی is negative past form 'you did not open/unfold/reveal' (ن‑ + شکفتی). Together: 'you did not reveal the choicest jewel of your nature.'
segments.s684-g9: نبیند is an archaic/classical negative-present (verb before subject) meaning “(does) not see / cannot see.” Here the usual SOV order is inverted: verb نبیند appears first, followed by the subject کسی (used in the sense “no one”), then the definite object شاه را (را marks the object). جز introduces the exception and attaches to the following clause بروم (“I go / I who go”) to mean “except me.”
segments.s684-g10: که is a relative pronoun meaning “who/that,” referring back to the excepted person (بروم). اکنون is an adverb “now.” کهن شد is a two-word predicate where کهن (old) is the adjective and شد (became) is the past/retrospective verb “became/grown”; together “has grown old.” بران is the contracted prepositional phrase بر آن “upon that,” and مرز وبوم is the locative phrase “border and homeland,” so the clause modifies the excepted person: “who has now grown old on that frontier and land.”
segments.s684-g11: کنون‌گر is an archaic conditional/discourse particle (کنون گر = “if now / but now”); it introduces the condition. دهیدم here is an older-form verbal sequence: دهید (2nd-person present/subjunctive/imperative “give/grant”) plus the enclitic ـم “to me,” so the phrase functions as “grant me / give me.” به جان is a sworn formula “by my life,” and زینهار is an exclamatory warning/plea (“zīnahār” = “I beg you, take heed / spare me”).
segments.s684-g12: بیایم is the first-person present/subjunctive used for a future or conditional action (“I will come / I shall come”). بر is the preposition “on/upon,” and پهلوان سوار is a noun phrase in which سوار describes being mounted; together it means “mounted on the champion” or “as a rider upon the hero.” The clause expresses the result: if you grant me (the plea), I will come mounted on the champion.
segments.s684-g13: بگویم is first-person present/subjunctive (or jussive) “I will say / I shall tell.” سخن is the direct object “words / speech.” هرچ is a free-relative “whatever,” and پرسد is third-person present-subjunctive/indicative “he asks” (classical style). زمن (ز + من) is the poetic preposition ز “from/of” + من “me,” so هرچ پرسد زمن = “whatever he asks of me.” Word order places verb بگویم first, then object and relative clause.
segments.s684-g14: ز is the poetic preposition “about / from.” کمی و بیشی is a fixed paired expression meaning “lack and excess / smallness and largeness” (i.e., deficiencies or surpluses, details of measure). آن انجمن is “that assembly/meeting” (آن = that, انجمن = assembly). Together this phrase specifies the topic of what will be answered: “about the deficiencies or excesses of that assembly.”
segments.s684-g15: وگرنه is the conditional/contrastive conjunction “otherwise.” بپوشم is first-person present/subjunctive/optative of پوشیدن with the enclitic ـم: “I will put on / I would don.” سلیح نبرد is a noun phrase where سلیح “weapon/armament” is determined by نبرد “of battle,” so together “the weapons of battle” (i.e. war-gear) — the object of بپوشم.
segments.s684-g16: به جنگ اندر آیم is a locative verb phrase: به جنگ = “to/into battle,” اندر = “into/within,” and آیم is first-person present/subjunctive/intentional “I will enter / I will come in.” بکردار is an agentive/adjectival formation (ب‑ + کردار) meaning roughly “man/one of deed” or “one of action/valour,” and گرد here is an archaic verb-form (from گشتن) used to indicate a change/becoming — together conveying “become a man of deeds” or “turn to action.”
segments.s684-g1: چوروی زمین is a genitive phrase 'چوروی (thief/plunderer) of زمین (the earth)'; گشت is the past form of شدن 'became', and خورشید فام is a compound adjectival phrase 'sun‑hued' (خورشید used attributively + فام 'hue'). Word order is Subject (چوروی زمین) + predicate (گشت + predicate phrase).
segments.s684-g2: سخن گوی بندوی is a nominal group meaning 'the speaker/voice of the bondman/slave' (سخن 'speech' with گوی forming 'speaker/one who utters it', بندوی 'of the bondman'); برشد is a past (or perfect) form meaning 'rose up' (the voice rose); ببام is an archaic/prepositional phrase indicating upward reach 'to the roof/roof‑top' (here marking the direction to which the voice rose).
segments.s684-g3: ببهرام is the dative/indirect object formed with به + proper name (here 'to Bahram'); گفت is the past form 'said' (subject is omitted and understood from context); ای جهاندیده مرد is a vocative: ای introduces direct address, جهاندیده is a preposed adjective 'worldly‑wise', and مرد is the noun 'man'.
segments.s684-g4: برانگه is an archaic adverb 'thereupon / at that moment'; که introduces a subordinate clause ('when/that'); برخاست is the past of برخاستن 'rose, departed'; از دشت گرد is a prepositional phrase 'from the plain around' where گرد functions adjectivally as 'around/surrounding'.
segments.s684-g5: چو here is the temporal subordinating particle 'when'; خسرو is the subject 'Khosrow/the king'; شما را is the object 'you' marked by the object marker را; بدید is the past 'saw'; او is the third‑person pronoun 'he' resumed for clarity; برفت is the past of رفتن 'he departed/left' (the printed senses given do not include this meaning).
segments.s684-g6: سوی روم is a directional phrase 'toward Rome' (سوی + place); با لشکر خویش is 'with his army' where خویش ('own/related') plus the enclitic ش marks 'his own'; the final تفت in the manuscript here functions as the past verb 'رفت' 'went/advanced' in sense (i.e. 'he went with his army'), but the senses given in the data do not reflect that meaning.
segments.s684-g7: کنون‌گر is a poetic contraction meaning 'کنون اگر' = 'now if'; تو is the 2nd‑person singular 'you'; پران is an adjective 'winged' (پَرّان, 'having wings' / 'taking wing'); شوی is the 2nd‑person present/subjunctive of شدن 'you become' (used here for a hypothetical: 'if you should become'); چون introduces a simile 'like', modifying عقاب 'eagle'.
segments.s684-g8: وگر is the alternative conditional 'or if'; برتر is 'higher/superior' used adverbially here; آری in this context is an archaic/poetic verbal form (2nd‑person) meaning 'you raise/lift' (not the interjection 'yes'); سر از آفتاب is 'your head above the sun' (سر 'head/top' + از 'from/above' + آفتاب 'sun').
segments.s684-g41: This clause is inverted: تو (you) is the subject, با خسرو (with Khosrow) marks union/association, یکی (one) is the complement 'one (person/partner)'. شوم گشتی here is a fused predicate meaning 'became one' (شوم as adjectival/qualifying element and گشتی as the past-tense verb 'you became/got'); the phrase literally 'you with Khosrow became one.' Word order (subject–adjunct–predicate–complement) is typical of older/poetic Persian.
segments.s684-g42: جهاندیده‌ای is an adjectival/nominal phrase 'a worldly-wise one' (with the -ی ending); کردی is the past tense 'you did/you showed' and takes جهاندیده‌ای as its object/description. از کودکی is an ablative temporal phrase 'from childhood' modifying کردی. Together: 'you acted/showed yourself a man of the world from childhood.'
segments.s684-g43: کنون is an adverb 'now'; آمدی is the 2nd-person past 'you came/you have come.' با + دلی پر سخن is an instrumental/attendant phrase 'with a heart full of speech/words' (دلی here functions as 'heart/feeling' and پر + noun = 'full of ...'). The whole clause: 'Now you have come with a heart full of words.'
segments.s684-g44: که introduces a content clause 'that …' with من as the subject. نو کنم uses نو (new) + کنم (subjunctive/present of کردن used here to express intention/claim: 'I will make/ I may make') and is followed by روزگار کهن 'the old/ancient age' as object. Literal order: 'that I (novo) make the age ancient new' → 'that I will make the ancient age new.'
segments.s684-g45: بدو (to him) + گفت (said) places the indirect object first: 'to him said Banduy' — a common poetic inversion; بندوی is the speaker's name/title. کای is the vocative particle 'O', and سرفراز is the vocative epithet 'proud/high‑famed.' Together this opens the speech: 'Banduy said to him: O proud one...'
segments.s684-g46: زمن is the contracted prepositional phrase ز + من 'from me'; راستی جوی is an imperative 'seek truth' (راستی = truth; جوی = seek/search). و links the next admonition: تندی مساز is a negative prohibitive 'do not make/do not be hasty' (مساز = 'do not make/produce' used idiomatically for 'do not act hastily'). The clause thus contains two imperatives addressed to the addressee.
segments.s684-g47: بدان is the imperative 'know' (ب- prefix + دان 'know'); کان is an archaic/demonstrative 'that' (introducing a substantive clause). شهنشاه خویش ش منست parses as 'the shahanshah (is) my own' with خویش (here split as خوی + ش) meaning 'own/his', and منست functioning as the copular/predicative element ('is mine' in context). This is an archaic/poetic syntactic shape common in Ferdowsi.
segments.s684-g48: بزرگیش 'his greatness' (بزرگی + ش), ورادیش 'his coming/arrival' (وراد + ش), and پیش منست 'are before me' (پیش = before/in the presence of; منست as the predicate). The two possessive nouns are coordinated implicitly and predicated to be 'before me' — 'his greatness and his coming stand before me.'
segments.s684-g17: چو begins a temporal subordinate clause meaning "when." The finite verb بشنید is past tense "he heard." ز + و (written here as separate tokens ز و) together mean "from him" (ز = from, و here represents the clitic pronoun 'him'), and این سخن is the direct object "this speech/these words." Word order is temporal marker → subject → verb → source → object.
segments.s684-g18: دل مرد is the subject phrase "the man's/hero's heart" (دل + appositive/identifier مرد). برنا شد is a predicative phrase: برنا ("youthful/renewed, vigorous") + شد (past of شدن, "became/was made"). از غم کهن is a causal/ablative phrase "from/because of old/ancient grief" modifying the state change.
segments.s684-g19: به یاران is the indirect-object prepositional phrase "to/for the companions." چنین is an adverb "thus/so," and گفت is the past-tense verb "he said." کاکنون (کا + اکنون) is an emphatic temporal "but/now" introducing the rhetorical question چه سود "what use/what profit." The clause is a main clause followed by a rhetorical question.
segments.s684-g20: اگر introduces a conditional clause "if." من is the first‑person pronoun "I." برآرم is a present-subjunctive/optative first‑person form (here translating as "I were to raise / I should raise") from برآوردن "raise/lift up." ز indicates source "from," بندوی is "the bondman/servant/captive," and دود is the noun "smoke." The whole is a conditional subordinate clause answering the rhetorical چه سود.
segments.s684-g21: همان به که is an idiomatic phrase meaning roughly "it is best/it is fitting that" and introduces a purposive/subjunctive clause. او را is the definite direct object "him" (را marks definiteness/emphasis). برپهلوان here functions as a goal phrase "to the champion" (بر + پهلوان), with the main verb postponed to the next segment.
segments.s684-g22: برم is the first‑person singular present/subjunctive (used here with future/intentive force) of بردن "take/carry" meaning "I will take/carry." هم is an emphatic/augmentive particle "indeed/even/also." برین is a contracted form of بر این "in this; thus" (بر + ین where ین = variant of این), followed by گونه "manner" giving "in this manner." روشن‌روان is an epithet/adjective "bright‑souled/cheerful" that qualifies the manner or addresses the addressee. The sequence gives the action and the manner in which it will be done.
segments.s684-g23: بگوید is a third‑person subjunctive/optative form here used jussively/imperatively in indirect speech—"let him tell" or "tell him." بدو is the dative "to him." هرچ داند is a free relative clause "whatever he knows" (هرچ = whatever; داند = he knows). ز شاه is the prepositional phrase "about/concerning the king" (ز = concerning/of).
segments.s684-g24: This is a compact conditional-proverbial line. اگر marks the condition "if." سر is the noun "head" (direct object of دادن if expanded). The form دهد‌گر stands for a conditional verbal idea "if he gives" (دهد = he gives; the -گر/-گرِ particle here flips the condition into proverbial formulaic wording), and ستاند is a past/future archaic form meaning "seizes/takes." کلاه is "hat/cap." The clause thus says proverbially that "if he gives his head, the taker will take (even) his hat."

sentences.s684-s29: He remained until the sun was hidden; with his heart full of thought he fell asleep.
sentences.s684-s25: I have sacrificed my life and stood firm; if you are superior, do not be crooked.
sentences.s684-s26: Bahram said to him, “For this sin that you committed, I will not bring you to ruin.”
sentences.s684-s27: But you, too, will soon be slain by his hand; and when you are summoned, tell me the truth.
sentences.s684-s28: They placed a fetter on the captive’s feet, and they gave him to Bahram for protection from harm.
sentences.s684-s13: He said to the bondman, 'O wretched seeker of remedy, you — tell these judgments to Bahram.'
sentences.s684-s14: A lion came down from the bondman's roof; the renowned, brave man rode it.
sentences.s684-s15: On hearing (this), Bahram came with an army; Khosrow set out toward Rome, desirous of vengeance.
sentences.s684-s16: Zupur was greatly angered against Siavash; to him he said, 'O pale, ill‑fated one!'
sentences.s684-s17: That was not your doing; now I have told/commanded you — I, unskilled and astonished, praised you.
sentences.s684-s18: He summoned the ambitious bondman before him; Bahram's anger then drove him away.
sentences.s684-s19: He said to him, 'O ill-bodied, ill-doer!' — (and) he reproached the deceitful man at the gate.
sentences.s684-s20: You bewildered my host; you did not reveal the choicest jewel of your nature.
sentences.s684-s5: No one can see the king except me, who has now grown old on that border and homeland.
sentences.s684-s6: If you now grant me this — by my life, I beg — I will come mounted on the champion.
sentences.s684-s7: I will tell whatever he asks of me about the lack or excess of that assembly.
sentences.s684-s8: Otherwise I will don the weapons of battle and enter the fight, becoming a man of deeds.
sentences.s684-s1: The earth’s plunderer turned sun‑colored; the slave’s voice rose up to the roof.
sentences.s684-s2: He said to Bahram: 'O worldly‑wise man — at that moment when he rose from the surrounding plain...'
sentences.s684-s3: When Khosrow saw you, he set out toward Rome with his army.
sentences.s684-s4: Now if you take wing like an eagle — or if you could lift your head above the sun.
sentences.s684-s21: You became one with Khosrow; you have behaved like a worldly-wise man since childhood.
sentences.s684-s22: Now you have come with a heart full of words, saying that I will make the ancient age new.
sentences.s684-s23: To him Banduy said: 'O proud one, seek truth from me and do not be hasty.'
sentences.s684-s24: Know this: that shahanshah is mine; his greatness and his coming are before me.
sentences.s684-s9: When Bahram heard these words from him, the hero's heart was revived from an old grief.
sentences.s684-s10: He said to his companions thus: "But now what use is it if I raise smoke from the bondman?"
sentences.s684-s11: It is better that I bring him to the champion; I will carry him off in this same manner, O bright‑souled one.
sentences.s684-s12: Tell him whatever he knows about the king; if he gives his head, the taker will take his hat.
